note: Mathlib/Topology/Sion.lean:336-340, `theorem minimax` proves inf_sup = sup_inf (Sion's minimax equality) for quasiconcave/quasiconvex functions on convex sets. File docstring: 'As a particular case, one get the von Neumann theorem where f is bilinear and E, F are finite dimensional' — a strict generalization of the classical von Neumann minimax theorem the Wikidata 'Minimax' article centers on, not a specialization of it.

Mathlib/MeasureTheory/Integral/IntervalIntegral/TrapezoidalRule.lean:32-34, `noncomputable def trapezoidal_integral` plus `trapezoidal_error_le` (convergence/error bound, line 18) formalize the trapezoidal rule, one specific numerical-integration method. Decl already carries @[wikidata Q833293] (Trapezoidal rule); it is a narrower special case of the broader 'Numerical integration' field concept, for which no general quadrature/Simpson's-rule/Monte-Carlo-integration decl exists in Mathlib.
